patch.value: CONTEXT: pm enforces quality on a fast-moving, agent-edited codebase; naive 100% coverage is impractical for large branchy command files (create/update/settings/item-store). DECISION: keep global V8 coverage thresholds at 100% but maintain a curated vitest.config.ts include/exclude dual-list — small pure-logic modules go in include and must be fully covered; large complex command files are listed in exclude (often also in include) so they are not gated; new behaviour is extracted into small src/core/** modules added to include. A static-quality-gate caps tests/unit file count to keep the suite navigable. CONSEQUENCES: high real coverage where it is cheap; pragmatic exemptions are explicit and reviewable; contributors must learn the dual-list pattern and the file-count cap; adding a command means adding its pure logic to a covered core module. EVIDENCE: vitest.config.ts include/exclude; scripts/release/static-quality-gate.mjs (--max-files-per-dir); backlog pm-othr (doc), pm-wc0d (cap governance test), pm-zyse (wiring checklist).
ADR: Test-coverage governance — 100% V8 thresholds with a curated include/exclude allowlist, a tests/unit file-count cap, and pure-logic extraction into small core modules
Test-coverage governance: 100% V8 thresholds with a curated include/exclude allowlist, a tests/unit file-count cap, and pure-logic extraction into small core modules
Recorded as an ADR for a decision already in effect; appended to the living ADR log (pm-m2u1).
